title: Whole user-space parity recovery and authority cutover plan
owner: Governance Board / LifeOS Data Agent / QA Agent
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
scope_principle: This is a DEFINITION artifact. It defines the staged migration that must prove the entire user-space is represented, reconstructable, recoverable, and operated from PostgreSQL BEFORE one explicit owner-approved authority switch retires flat files as editable authority. No source is deleted, no history is lost, no cutover is irreversible, and no production state is mutated by this task. Flat files are not destroyed at cutover: they transition from editable authority to (a) the deterministic derived projection produced by envctl from PostgreSQL rows and (b) a preserved read-only source reference retained until a separate retention approval.

store_001_alignment.single_durable_writer: PostgreSQL owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class.
store_001_alignment.forward_only_sync: redb -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL; no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write.
store_001_alignment.verified_cutover_mechanism: A class leaves flat-file / redb-durable authority only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run under one SHA-256 content identity (codedb_store_pg::PgStore implements the same BlobStore contract). The guardrail is 'no unverified cutover', never 'flat files stay canonical'.
store_001_alignment.upgrades_not_downgrades: Retiring flat files as editable authority in favor of PostgreSQL is building to the intended blueprint spec; no functionality is removed because the projection path (envctl PostgreSQL -> filesystem) reproduces byte-identical source directories and the preserved source remains available until retention approval.
store_001_alignment.no_silent_reclassification: The two current durable redb uses (PGREDB-001 CodeDB source_blobs, PGREDB-002 envctl migration_db) are migrated only through the BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover, never by assumption; they are enumerated explicitly in the class set below.

user_space_class_set.description: Every user-space class named in the POSTGRES-002 model gate and the STORE-001 state-class decision is assigned exactly one canonical target owner and one authority_action. Classes 1-14 currently hold flat-file (or redb-durable) editable authority and switch to PostgreSQL at the single Stage-4 switch. Classes 15-18 retain their STORE-001 owner and DO NOT switch (they are not flat-file editable authority).

switching_classes.class: file content bytes / captured source blobs
switching_classes.source_representation: flat files on disk; today also codedb_store_redb durable blobs (PGREDB-001)
switching_classes.identity_key: SHA-256 content hash of file bytes (byte-exact)
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: BlobStore SHA-256 parity contract (codedb_store_pg::PgStore + blobstore_parity.rs) — verified per-class swap under one content identity

switching_classes.class: directory tree structure and module_path metadata
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: envctl stores module_path metadata; projection reconstructs the exact directory structure (blueprint materialization step 2)

switching_classes.class: code modules / AST fragments / raw_code rows
switching_classes.identity_key: module_path + SHA-256 of the raw_code block
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: Nushell AST parse -> redb buffer -> envctl bridge -> PostgreSQL rows (blueprint three pillars of ingestion); edits become SQL UPDATE on rows
collected and ingested per POSTGRES-004 lossless ingestion contract

switching_classes.class: planning and control tables (task graph, North Star, architecture, decisions, provenance, proofs, status)
switching_classes.source_representation: flat planning-spine files (task_graph.source.csv, proof_records/**, north_star_coverage.source.csv, task_families/**)
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: POSTGRES-003 database-native control tables with deterministic projection contract; every planning question queried, every accepted answer updated in the database
collected and ingested per POSTGRES-004; lossless round-trip keys per POSTGRES-002 model

switching_classes.class: LifeOS application state (persisted settings and state)
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: canonical state read/directed/advanced via the POSTGRES-007 LifeOS Mission Control UDS path
BlobStore SHA-256 parity contract (same content identity as USC-FILES)
stable identities defined by the POSTGRES-002 relational graph and identity model
permission records modeled per POSTGRES-002; access decisions reconstructable

switching_classes.class: history, temporal relations, and provenance lineage
switching_classes.source_representation: flat-file history (git lineage, source commit/PR provenance)
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: temporal relations per POSTGRES-002; PITR recovery per Architecture Context Matrix (PostgreSQL persistence = Enterprise ACID / PITR); source commit/PR lineage preserved (adopted_capabilities.source.csv)

switching_classes.class: relationships / graph edges / causal dependency edges
switching_classes.source_representation: implicit in flat-file imports and cross-references
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: causal dependency edges committed alongside AST fragments and embeddings (blueprint PostgreSQL knowledge-graph)

switching_classes.source_representation: not present in flat files; derived at ingestion
switching_classes.identity_key: (source row identity, embedding model, model version, config)
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: embeddings produced by a local model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) during the envctl bridge and stored in PostgreSQL; reproducible re-embedding is the recovery path
switching_classes.gated_on_open_dependency: Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation must be proven locally before this class fully cuts over (STORE-001 open dependency; NBVERIFY-001 / REDB-CLAIM-023 remainder). Until then embeddings may be produced via the proven Bun-loaded ruvector N-API path and committed to PostgreSQL.

switching_classes.class: envctl migration/orchestration state (targets, runs, run_events, approvals, checkpoints, rollbacks)
switching_classes.canonical_target_owner: PostgreSQL audit/history tables (one-way promotion of run history)
switching_classes.migration_mechanism: one-way promote run history to PostgreSQL audit tables via verified parity; envctl's replayable migration engine re-derives state on recovery

non_switching_classes.class: transient high-frequency state (agent scratchpad thoughts, terminal traces, intermediate logs, BLAKE3 dedup cache, application-level WAL/outbox, shared UI read-view)
non_switching_classes.reason: This is redb's intended terminal role (blueprint: local AI scratchpad / high-frequency write buffer / dedup cache / WAL / geometry engine). It is not flat-file editable authority; only significant states flush forward to PostgreSQL via envctl. No authority switch applies.

non_switching_classes.class: per-agent cognition (MicroLoRA adapters, SONA policy matrices, RL weights) in AgentDB .rvf
non_switching_classes.reason: AgentDB is the intended owner of active cognition; non-authoritative for shared macro-state and witnessed by Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256) rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors. Not flat-file authority; no switch.

non_switching_classes.class: derived filesystem projections (materialized source directories for musl compilation, exported release trees)
non_switching_classes.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL (source of truth) projected read-only by envctl / CodeDB materialization
non_switching_classes.reason: After the switch, flat directories are the deterministic derived OUTPUT of envctl projection from PostgreSQL rows, regenerated from module_path metadata; they are disposable and never a competing writable owner (PGAUTH-004 invariant). This is what 'retiring flat files as editable authority while retaining projections' means.

non_switching_classes.class: external secrets / provenance authority (API keys, attestation bundles, source pins)
non_switching_classes.reason: PostgreSQL stores references/provenance, never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets. Not flat-file editable authority; no switch.

parity_dimensions.check: For each switching class, COUNT(*) of ingested PostgreSQL rows equals the source inventory count produced by the POSTGRES-004 archive-first collectors.
parity_dimensions.evidence: per-class count table (source inventory count vs PostgreSQL row count)
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: exact per-class equality across all 14 switching classes; zero missing, zero extra

parity_dimensions.check: Every source stable identity (SHA-256 blob hash, module_path, row/task/anchor/proof identity, edge identity) maps to exactly one PostgreSQL identity and back (bijective).
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: bijective identity map; zero orphans and zero collisions

parity_dimensions.check: Byte-exact content parity: SHA-256 of every PostgreSQL-stored blob equals the SHA-256 of the source blob under the BlobStore parity contract.
parity_dimensions.evidence: blobstore_parity.rs green run against a disposable PostgreSQL (STORE-001 durable-class cutover proof)
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: all content hashes equal; exact-byte materialization holds

parity_dimensions.check: Non-text metadata (mode/permissions bits, timestamps, size, mime, module_path, ordering) preserved per the POSTGRES-004 content-and-metadata checksum contract.
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: metadata checksum equal for every entity

parity_dimensions.check: Graph edges (containment, dependency, causal) reconstructed in PostgreSQL match the source-derived edge set by endpoints and type.
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: edge-set equality; no dangling or invented edges (Causal Graph Verification consistent)

parity_dimensions.check: Permission/ACL records reconstruct identical access decisions for a representative principal x resource matrix.
Temporal/provenance history preserved; PostgreSQL PITR reconstructs any prior committed state; source commit/PR lineage retained.
parity_dimensions.evidence: history-depth comparison + a PITR restore-to-timestamp verification
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: history depth equals source; PITR restore reproduces the target prior state; lineage intact

parity_dimensions.check: RuVector hybrid search (ORDER BY semantic_embedding <->) returns the same top-k target functions as a reference query set; embeddings are reproducible.
parity_dimensions.evidence: retrieval parity report over a fixed reference query set + reproducible re-embedding checksum
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: top-k retrieval parity within defined tolerance; re-embedding deterministic
parity_dimensions.gated_on_open_dependency: Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation (STORE-001 open dependency)

parity_dimensions.check: Observed LifeOS behavior driven from PostgreSQL equals behavior from flat-file authority for the bounded slice (extends the POSTGRES-009 source-to-DB-to-projection-to-build-to-UDS round trip).
parity_dimensions.evidence: behavior diff between flat-file-authoritative run and PostgreSQL-authoritative run
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: behavior diff empty for the bounded slice

parity_dimensions.check: envctl projection from PostgreSQL reproduces a byte-identical source directory (POSTGRES-008 deterministic projection; repeated projections byte-identical).
parity_dimensions.evidence: SHA-256 of projected directory tree vs source directory tree; repeat-projection determinism check
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: projection SHA-256 equals source; repeated projections byte-identical

parity_dimensions.check: A full backup of the PostgreSQL authority (relational + vectors + provenance) is captured and checksummed before the switch.
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: backup exists and its checksum is recorded prior to Stage 4

parity_dimensions.check: Restoring from that backup into a disposable namespace yields identical database state.
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: restored-state checksum equals backup source checksum

parity_dimensions.check: The rehearsed failback to preserved source authority succeeds: database writes stop and flat-file authority resumes with no data loss and both representations intact.
parity_dimensions.evidence: failback rehearsal record on a disposable namespace
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: failback rehearsal green; both representations retained; zero unarchived destructive action

parity_dimensions.check: LifeOS Mission Control (POSTGRES-007 UDS path) reads, directs, and advances the canonical PostgreSQL state live, with audit events.
parity_dimensions.evidence: captured UDS request/response answered from canonical DB + emitted audit event
parity_dimensions.pass_criterion: UDS round trip answered from canonical PostgreSQL; audit event recorded; no hidden flat-file control-plane detour

migration_stages.name: Parity decomposition
migration_stages.objective: Prove every switching user-space class is represented and reconstructable in PostgreSQL.
migration_stages.entry_gate: Graph closure past POSTGRES-009; bounded round trip proven.
migration_stages.exit_gate: PD-01 entity_counts, PD-02 identities, PD-03 bytes, PD-04 metadata, PD-05 relationships, PD-06 permissions, PD-07 history, PD-08 semantic_retrieval, PD-10 reconstruction all green across all switching classes.
migration_stages.authority_state: flat files remain the editable authority (no switch)

migration_stages.name: Restore rehearsal
migration_stages.objective: Prove the recovery envelope before any switch.
migration_stages.exit_gate: PD-11 backup, PD-12 restore, PD-13 failback all green on a disposable namespace; both representations retained.
migration_stages.authority_state: flat files remain the editable authority (no switch)

migration_stages.name: Staged dual-read
migration_stages.objective: Serve reads from PostgreSQL alongside flat-file authority and measure operational parity.
migration_stages.exit_gate: PD-09 behavior and PD-14 observed_lifeos_operation green for the bounded slice while flat files are still the editable authority.
migration_stages.authority_state: flat files remain the editable authority; PostgreSQL is read-shadowed, not yet authoritative

migration_stages.name: Explicit authority switch
migration_stages.objective: Perform ONE explicit owner-approved switch of editable authority from flat files to PostgreSQL.
migration_stages.entry_gate: ALL 14 parity dimensions (PD-01..PD-14) green across all switching classes AND STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant satisfied (green live blobstore_parity.rs for durable classes; RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation for the semantic class).
migration_stages.exit_gate: A single explicit owner-approved authority switch is recorded; flat files become the read-only preserved source reference and the envctl-derived projection; edits now flow to PostgreSQL rows (SQL UPDATE).
migration_stages.authority_state: PostgreSQL is the editable authority; flat files are preserved read-only reference + derived projection

migration_stages.name: Retention decision
migration_stages.objective: After observed-stable operation, a separate governance retention approval decides preserved-source disposition.
migration_stages.entry_gate: Post-switch operation observed stable via PD-14.
migration_stages.exit_gate: An explicit retention approval decision is recorded; until then the preserved source is retained and never deleted.
migration_stages.authority_state: PostgreSQL authoritative; preserved source retained until retention approval

preconditions: All 14 parity dimensions PD-01..PD-14 green across all switching classes
STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant satisfied: green live blobstore_parity.rs against a disposable PostgreSQL for durable classes
RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation proven for the semantic-embeddings class (open dependency)
authority_switch_gate.effect: Editable authority moves from flat files to PostgreSQL; flat files become read-only preserved reference and envctl-derived projection.
authority_switch_gate.irreversibility: none — the switch is reversible via the failback plan until retention approval retires the preserved source

retention_policy.rule: Preserved source remains available (read-only) until an explicit retention approval decision.
retention_policy.until_approval: The preserved source and the failed/superseded cutover namespaces are retained; neither representation is deleted.

rollback_failback_plan.trigger: Any parity dimension fails, the switch is not approved, or post-switch operation is unstable.
actions: Fail back to preserved source authority
Retain the failed cutover namespace and its proof without deleting either representation
rollback_failback_plan.guarantee: No source deletion, no history loss, no irreversible cutover; both the flat-file and PostgreSQL representations survive every failure path.

invariants: One explicit authority switch only, and only after all 14 parity dimensions pass.
Single durable writer PostgreSQL; redb never becomes a competing durable writer (STORE-001).
Forward-only sync redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL; no dual-write.
Verified cutover under one SHA-256 identity; never by assumption (BlobStore parity contract).
Preserved source retained until retention approval; never deleted by this task.
Every user-space class is assigned exactly one canonical owner and one authority_action.
open_dependencies: Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation is required before the semantic-embeddings class (USC-SEMANTIC-EMBEDDINGS) fully cuts over (STORE-001 open dependency; NBVERIFY-001 / REDB-CLAIM-023 remainder).
Live blobstore_parity.rs green run against a disposable PostgreSQL captures the durable-class cutover proof (STORE-001 open dependency).

non_execution.statement: This artifact defines the staged migration only. No database was provisioned or mutated, no schema was deployed, no flat file was retired, and no authority was switched. Authority remains unchanged (next_action: keep authority unchanged).
